title: moraea luteoalba
intro: Moraea luteoalba, also known as the White Formed-leaved Cape Gladiolus, is a species of flowering plant in the Iridaceae family, native to the Western Cape Province of South Africa. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Moraea luteoalba include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Moraea luteoalba is traditionally prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it can also be used as a poultice or applied topically to the affected area.
